title: Object 300

» SUBVOID FRAGMENTS «
  ✎ '''Overview'''
Object 300, formally designated by the '''[[V.C.U.]]''' as '''Subvoid Fragments''' are anomalous shards or fragments of corrupted matter that have undergone incomplete '''[[Nullification|nullification]]''', where a material object or geometry has only been partially erased by exposure to '''[[The Void]]'''. Visually, they resemble corrupted fragments of wood, metal, or any other structural materials, laced with flickering static and distortion artifacts. Most instances of '''Subvoid Fragments''' take on rough cuboid shapes, albeit with multiple imperfections such as jagged edges and bulging faces. '''Subvoid Fragments''' are highly unstable, and appear to exhibit fluctuations in temperature, weight, and density. Their stability mainly depends on how long they have been exposed to The Void before being taken out of exposure range, either due to no-clipping or being taken. As a result of their instability, '''Subvoid Fragments''' can sometimes be hazardous, due to their heat or complexion. '''Subvoid Fragments''' barely resemble the object they previously were. A sample was viewed through a microscope, and researchers noted the molecular structure of the object was completely damaged, with inconsistencies never seen in any normal object. '''Subvoid Fragments''' have to be contained within special containment cells to prevent them from no-clipping away or disintegrating.
  ✎ '''Uses'''
'''Subvoid Fragments''' barely have any notable use, however, their most prominent use was in detecting nullification risks in various levels. '''Subvoid Fragments''' will become more unstable the closer they are to The Void. As a result of this, the V.C.U. has used them to detect the risks of nullification in multiple levels. More unstable fragments will indicate the level is at higher risk of exposure to The Void. Agents of the V.C.U. will usually carry '''Subvoid Fragments''' in capsules where they cannot harm the holder. A built-in device in the capsule will detect how unstable the fragment is. '''Subvoid Fragments''' have also been used by the '''V.C.U.''' to further investigate the effects of nullification, and has been key in the documentation of the phenomenon.
  ✎ '''Formation and Containment'''
'''Subvoid Fragments''' are almost impossible to come across due to the exceptionally specific conditions in which they are made. As previously mentioned above, they derive from when a material object or geometry undergoes an incomplete process of '''Nullification'''. This occurs when matter is exposed to '''The Void''', and is in the middle of erasure, but is abruptly removed before the full process can take place, usually as a result of no-clipping. The result is an unstable fragment of their original form, with several aspects of their structure being altered. The formation of '''Subvoid Fragments''' is almost entirely unpredictable, making their appearance extremely rare. When one is discovered, high-ranking '''V.C.U.''' agents are instructed to secure it using specialized containment capsules designed to suppress minor spatial distortions and prevent further decay. All recovered fragments are then transported directly to '''Base Equilibrium''' for analysis under strict containment conditions.
  ✎ '''History'''
The first instance of a '''Subvoid Fragment''' was discovered in early 2019, when the remains of the search team sent out to investigate [[Incident 04-21-18]] retrieved a sample brought from [[Level 630|Level -6]]. This sample was researched for months after, and researchers linked it's behavior to the effects of incomplete nullification. Since then, only 5 more '''Subvoid Fragments''' have been discovered ''and'' brought back to '''Base Equilibrium''', to which each of them are kept in containment for research. Due to the immense rarity of these fragments, efforts to intentionally recreate the conditions for their formation remain unsuccessful.
  ✎ '''Documentation'''

|body=
'''- The following is footage of the retrieval of the first Subvoid Fragment, 2019.'''

- "How did the expedition go?"
- "Horribly."
- "...Shit. What the hell happened?"
- "We entered Level -6. You can guess what happened after that."
- "Oh god. Oh god.. I'll report this to the-"
- "We found some.. interesting samples though."
- "..Thatcher. What the fuck is that?"
- "I don't know. But I found it lying on the ground near a puddle of [[Object 3|nuclear infection]]."
- "That thing clearly shows instability. Very strange how it has kept itself together. This is no normal sample."
- "Hey- careful. It heats up all of the sudden sometimes. I've had to put it in a capsule, and the glass literally cracked from its heat."
- "How does this... thing, make it here? This is some whole [[The Broken|Broken]] type anomaly."
- "I don't know. I don't know. I think it might just be a step closer to finding out what happened."
- "..You think this is the culprit? This doesn't even have a- a constant shape!"
- "You really can't doubt stuff with how things happen in the Backrooms."
- "..."
- "So?"
- "I don't know. Just.. take it up with the research team or something."
- "On another note, I think there are worse things to deal with." }}
